    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go", "symbol": "NewTypedRateLimitingQueue"},
    {"repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go", "symbol": "NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig"}
  ],
  "text": "The controller work queue with rate limiting is implemented in kubernetes/kubernetes in the staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue package. The core files are:\n\n1. **queue.go** (lines 224-251): Implements the Typed[T] struct with the fundamental queue operations:\n   - Add(item T): Marks item as needing processing\n   - Get(): Blocks until it can return an item to be processed\n   - Done(item T): Marks item as done processing\n\n2. **rate_limiting_queue.go** (lines 21-40): Defines RateLimitingInterface and TypedRateLimitingInterface[T] which extends the queue with rate-limited re-queuing:\n   - AddRateLimited(item T): Adds item after rate limiter says it's ok\n   - Forget(item T): Indicates item is finished being retried\n   - NumRequeues(item T): Returns how many times item was requeued\n\n3. **default_rate_limiters.go** (lines 79-149): Implements two main rate limiters:\n   - ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter (line 80, 84-90): Does simple baseDelay*2^<num-failures> backoff\n   - BucketRateLimiter (line 59, 62-77): Token bucket based rate limiting using golang.org/x/time/rate\n\n4. **delaying_queue.go** (lines 37-41): Defines TypedDelayingInterface[T] with AddAfter(item T, duration) for delayed re-queuing\n\nThe controller reconcile loop pattern using Add/Get/Done is demonstrated in:\n\n- **staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go**: The official sample controller shows the complete pattern:\n  - Line 122: Creates workqueue with rate limiter using NewTypedRateLimitingQueue\n  - Lines 110-113: Creates custom rate limiter combining ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter and BucketRateLimiter\n  - Lines 162-188: Run() method starts workers\n  - Lines 193-196: runWorker() calls processNextWorkItem in a loop\n  - Lines 200-236: processNextWorkItem() demonstrates the core pattern:\n    - Line 201: item, shutdown := c.workqueue.Get() - blocks until item available\n    - Line 214: defer c.workqueue.Done(objRef) - ensures cleanup\n    - Line 217: err := c.syncHandler(ctx, objRef) - processes the item\n    - Line 221: c.workqueue.Forget(objRef) - clears retry state on success\n    - Line 234: c.workqueue.AddRateLimited(objRef) - re-queues on error with backoff\n\n- **staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/examples/workqueue/main.go**: Simpler example showing the basic pattern:\n  - Lines 55-71: processNextItem() demonstrates Get/Done pattern\n  - Line 57: key, quit := c.queue.Get()\n  - Line 64: defer c.queue.Done(key)\n  - Lines 100: c.queue.Forget(key) on success\n  - Line 103: c.queue.AddRateLimited(key) on error\n\nMultiple controllers throughout kubernetes/kubernetes use this pattern including: namespace_controller, tokencleaner, pvprotection, endpointslice, certificates, and many others. The DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter (default_rate_limiters.go, lines 50-56) is the recommended default, combining both exponential failure backoff (5ms to 1000s) and a token bucket (10 qps, 100 bucket size) for maximum 10 queries per second overall rate limiting."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Kubernetes Controller Manager Work Queue and Rate Limiting

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the controller work queue with rate limiting: the RateLimitingInterface, the ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter, the BucketRateLimiter, and the controller reconcile loop that uses Add/Get/Done for queue management.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files related to the controller work queue with rate limiting in kubernetes/kubernetes. Let me start by discovering the repositories and searching for the relevant code.
Now let me create a comprehensive JSON file with all the findings. Let me first check what else might be relevant:
Now let me create a comprehensive answer file with all the findings:

Perfect! I've created a comprehensive `answer.json` file with all findings about the Kubernetes controller work queue and rate limiting implementation. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Files Located:

### Core Work Queue Implementation:
1. **queue.go** - Implements the basic queue with `Add()`, `Get()`, `Done()` operations
2. **rate_limiting_queue.go** - Implements `RateLimitingInterface` with `AddRateLimited()`, `Forget()`, `NumRequeues()`
3. **default_rate_limiters.go** - Implements the two main rate limiters:
   - `ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter` - exponential backoff per item
   - `BucketRateLimiter` - token bucket rate limiting
4. **delaying_queue.go** - Implements delayed re-queuing with `AddAfter()`

### Controller Examples:
- **sample-controller/controller.go** - Official sample showing the complete reconcile loop pattern
- **client-go/examples/workqueue/main.go** - Basic workqueue example
- Multiple production controllers (namespace, bootstrap, certificates, endpointslice, etc.)

## Controller Reconcile Loop Pattern:
The standard pattern demonstrated in sample-controller is:
1. `Get()` - blocks until item available
2. `Done()` - deferred cleanup
3. `syncHandler()` - process the item
4. `Forget()` on success or `AddRateLimited()` on error for retry with backoff

The answer file includes 23 relevant files, 12 key symbols, and 17 call chain functions that form the complete controller work queue implementation with detailed narrative explanation.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes$ rate limiting interface work queue
repo:^github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes$ controller reconcile loop Add Get Done
repo:^github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes$ type.*RateLimiter interface When Forget NumRequeues

AddRateLimited(item T): Adds item after rate limiter says it's ok\n   - Forget(item T): Indicates item is finished being retried\n   - NumRequeues(item T): Returns how many times item was requeued\n\n3. **default_rate_limiters.go** (lines 79-149): Implements two main rate limiters:\n   - ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter (line 80, 84-90): Does simple baseDelay*2^<num-failures> backoff\n   - BucketRateLimiter (line 59, 62-77): Token bucket based rate limiting using golang.org/x/time/rate\n\n4. **delaying_queue.go** (lines 37-41): Defines TypedDelayingInterface[T] with AddAfter(item T, duration) for delayed re-queuing\n\nThe controller reconcile loop pattern using Add/Get/Done is demonstrated in:\n\n- **staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go**: The official sample controller shows the complete pattern:\n  - Line 122: Creates workqueue with rate limiter using NewTypedRateLimitingQueue\n  - Lines 110-113: Creates custom rate limiter combining ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter and BucketRateLimiter\n  - Lines 162-188: Run() method starts workers\n  - Lines 193-196: runWorker() calls processNextWorkItem in a loop\n  - Lines 200-236: processNextWorkItem() demonstrates the core pattern:\n    - Line 201: item, shutdown := c.workqueue.Get() - blocks until item available\n    - Line 214: defer c.workqueue.Done(objRef) - ensures cleanup\n    - Line 217: err := c.syncHandler(ctx, objRef) - processes the item\n    - Line 221: c.workqueue.Forget(objRef) - clears retry state on success\n    - Line 234: c.workqueue.AddRateLimited(objRef) - re-queues on error with backoff\n\n- **staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/examples/workqueue/main.go**: Simpler example showing the basic pattern:\n  - Lines 55-71: processNextItem() demonstrates Get/Done pattern\n  - Line 57: key, quit := c.queue.Get()\n  - Line 64: defer c.queue.Done(key)\n  - Lines 100: c.queue.Forget(key) on success\n  - Line 103: c.queue.AddRateLimited(key) on error\n\nMultiple controllers throughout kubernetes/kubernetes use this pattern including: namespace_controller, tokencleaner, pvprotection, endpointslice, certificates, and many others. The DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter (default_rate_limiters.go, lines 50-56) is the recommended default, combining both exponential failure backoff (5ms to 1000s) and a token bucket (10 qps, 100 bucket size) for maximum 10 queries per second overall rate limiting."
}
